identity.title: Precision Rectifier Envelope Detector
identity.supported_scope: pure voltage-domain event-driven transient behavioral Verilog-A

parameters.name: vth
parameters.override_behavior: sets the clk and rst voltage-coded decision threshold

parameters.name: vcm
parameters.override_behavior: sets rectification common mode and envelope reset level

parameters.name: decay
parameters.override_behavior: sets the envelope decrement on each sampled decay update

parameters.name: tr
parameters.override_behavior: sets env and metric transition smoothing

properties.observable_contract: Rect equals vcm plus the absolute input deviation from vcm, so equal positive and negative excursions produce equal rectified levels, bounded to 0 V through 0.9 V.
Initialization or a rising clk update with rst active restores env to vcm and clears envelope memory.
At a rising clk update, a rectified value above the stored envelope is acquired immediately as the new env value.
When rect is below the stored envelope, each rising clk update lowers env by at most decay and never below rect or vcm.
Metric is high while env exceeds rect by more than 30 mV and low otherwise.

modeling_constraints: Drive rect as a continuous voltage-domain rectified observable and env/metric from event-updated state.
Use voltage contributions only; do not use current contributions, transistor-level devices, or AC/noise behavior.
Do not add validation-only ports, hooks, or simulator-specific side channels.
